judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit the core factual findings (a-d) nearly identically, but Report 1 more precisely captures the structural/ordering defect (e) — noting the remediation commitment is a single sentence buried at the end and explicitly recommending restructuring — while Report 2 only vaguely flags the rule-3 violation without pinpointing the buried remediation line or recommending a fix.

report_1.evidence: Per incidents/OPS-412-timeline.md, failures began at 14:03 and the first alert fired at 14:50 — a 47-minute detection gap

report_2.evidence: The timeline shows failures began at 14:03 and the first alert fired at 14:50 — a 47-minute detection gap

report_1.evidence: incidents/OPS-412-timeline.md records 1,284 failed transactions requiring manual reprocessing and 37 unrecoverable transactions that were refunded.

report_2.evidence: larkpay-comms/incidents/OPS-412-timeline.md records 1,284 failed transactions requiring manual reprocessing, of which 37 were unrecoverable and refunded.

report_1.evidence: April uptime was 99.77% (metrics/uptime-2026.csv), below the 99.9% contractual monthly SLA in docs/sla-commitments.md, which states any breach "owes service credits."

report_2.evidence: April 2026 uptime was 99.77% (metrics/uptime-2026.csv), below the 99.9% contractual monthly SLA in larkpay-comms/docs/sla-commitments.md, which states any such month "is a breach and owes service credits."

report_1.evidence: Blaming "the on-call engineer" violates style rule 1 ("Never assign blame to an individual", docs/customer-comms-style-guide.md). The sentence attributing the outage to an individual deploying an untested change must be rewritten to a blameless process framing.

report_2.evidence: "The outage was caused by the on-call engineer deploying an untested config change" directly violates rule 1 of larkpay-comms/docs/customer-comms-style-guide.md ("Never assign blame to an individual"). Rephrase in terms of process failure.

report_1.evidence: It opens with a reputation defense ("99.99% uptime... does not change our commitment") and buries impact; the closing paragraph is more self-praise. Restructure to lead with what customers experienced and what LarkPay is doing about it. ... the remediation is a single vague line ("a pre-deploy validation step") with no mention of fixing the 47-minute detection gap.

report_2.evidence: Structure violates rule 3 of the style guide ("Lead with customer impact and remediation"): the draft opens with a reliability boast and buries impact; remediation for affected customers (reprocessing/refunds per the timeline) is never mentioned.
